message.content:
{
  "items": [
    {
      "objective": "Negotiate a more favorable payment structure.",
      "leverage": "A phased payment structure can help mitigate risk for the Company by aligning payments with milestones.",
      "proposed_language": "For delivery on or before the delivery date specified in the Phase Agreement, Company shall pay to Developer 30% upon approval of specifications, 40% upon delivery of the software, and 30% upon successful completion of acceptance tests.",
      "rationale": "This structure ensures that payments are tied to specific deliverables, incentivizing the Developer to meet deadlines and quality standards.",
      "priority": "high",
      "source_page": 1,
      "source_section": "4.7",
      "source_clause_id": "ca82a980-4f6d-4df8-9bde-a0a43a31b0d4",
      "source_excerpt": "For delivery on or before the delivery date specified in the Phase Agreement, Company shall pay to Developer ……… [insert number] % of the price for that Phase.",
      "confidence": 0.85
    },
    {
      "objective": "Establish clear acceptance test criteria.",
      "leverage": "Defining specific acceptance criteria can prevent disputes and ensure that both parties have aligned expectations.",
      "proposed_language": "The Acceptance test will include specific performance metrics as detailed in Annex II, which must be met for the software to be considered acceptable.",
      "rationale": "Clear criteria will help both parties understand what constitutes a successful delivery, reducing the likelihood of disagreements.",
      "priority": "medium",
      "source_page": 1,
      "source_section": "4.4",
      "source_clause_id": "77912e9c-8858-4762-93f9-92bc1f63dbcb",
      "source_excerpt": "Upon approval of the programming specifications, Developer design, in conjunction with Company, an Acceptance test for these specifications. The Acceptance test will follow those standards listed in Annex II.",
      "confidence": 0.75
    },
    {
      "objective": "Reduce the cancellation period for delays.",
      "leverage": "A shorter cancellation period can provide the Company with more flexibility to terminate the agreement if the Developer fails to deliver on time.",
      "proposed_language": "In the event that Developer fails to deliver the completed programs within 1 month after the original delivery date, Company may cancel that Phase Agreement.",
      "rationale": "This change allows the Company to respond more quickly to delays, minimizing potential losses from unfulfilled contracts.",
      "priority": "medium",
      "source_page": 1,
      "source_section": "4.9",
      "source_clause_id": "fbca1200-9bae-4a0a-8990-7b76afcf59c1",
      "source_excerpt": "In the event that Developer fails to deliver the completed programs ………..[insert number] months after the original delivery date, and the delivery date was not modified, Company may cancel that Phase Agreement.",
      "confidence": 0.70
    }
  ]
}
